description: Behavioral eval suite for group-synastry. Each eval pairs a prompt with concrete, discriminating expectations. Reference positions in expectations are taken from validated Swiss Ephemeris computation (see reference-charts.json). Tolerances are stated explicitly per assertion. The reference subjects 'Alex' and 'Jordan' are fictional test personas with synthetic birth data.

truth_sources_doc._doc: Each eval below declares a `truth_sources` array listing the ephemerides/datasets whose output the eval's expectations check. Changing one of these sources should be expected to invalidate (or require re-tuning) the matching evals. Codes overlap with reference-charts.json's source_schema where applicable.

codes.swisseph_builtin: Swiss Ephemeris via pyswisseph 2.10 with Moshier fallback. Determines truth for Sun-Pluto, Lilith (mean apogee), nodes, ayanamsa, and houses/angles.
codes.swisseph_with_seas18: Swiss Ephemeris with seas_18.se1 installed (bundled in <skill>/ephe/ as of v1.2). Determines truth for arcsecond/arcminute-accurate Chiron, Ceres, Pallas, Juno, Vesta. lib/ephem.py raises EphemerisFileMissing if a body whose best source is this code is requested while the file is absent.
codes.keplerian_jpl_j2000: JPL J2000 osculating Keplerian elements via lib/kepler.py. Best source for (136199) Eris (no bundled Swiss Eph file). Acceptable fallback for Chiron/Ceres/Pallas/Juno/Vesta via lib.ephem.calc_body(..., force_source='keplerian_jpl_j2000') when reproducibility across machines without seas_18.se1 matters; not used as a silent fallback.
codes.zoneinfo: Python's zoneinfo module (IANA tzdata). Determines truth for historical-DST resolution (e.g. CDT/MST/PST/etc. in past years).
codes.schema: File-format correctness for people.json and settings.json. No ephemeris involvement.
codes.derived: Computed from other inputs in the eval (midpoints, age arithmetic, calendar conversions). No external ephemeris.
codes.behavior: Skill-behavior assertions (clarifying questions, output-format announcements, refusal to fabricate). No truth data involved.

evals.prompt: Add Alex to my group: born June 15, 1988 at 8:20 AM in Chicago, Illinois (41.88°N, 87.63°W, CDT)
evals.expected_output: people.json updated with Alex; tz stored as IANA name 'America/Chicago' which correctly resolves to CDT (UTC-5) for the birth date
expectations: people.json contains an entry with display_name 'Alex'

Alex's birth.tz is a valid IANA timezone identifier (specifically 'America/Chicago'). The bare abbreviation 'CDT' is not stored, because the schema requires IANA names so that historical DST is correctly applied.
When the stored tz is applied to '1988-06-15 08:20' local time, the resulting UTC offset is -05:00. This confirms CDT (daylight time) was correctly resolved for that June date.
Alex's birth.lat is 41.88 (positive, North) and birth.lon is -87.63 (negative for West).
Alex's birth.time_accuracy is set (one of: exact, approximate, noon-default, unknown). The skill either asked the user OR inferred from context that '8:20 AM' implies exact.

evals.prompt: Add Jordan to my group: born February 9, 1991 at 2:45 PM MST in Denver, Colorado (39.74°N, 104.99°W)
evals.expected_output: people.json updated; tz field is 'America/Denver'; system confirms MST (standard time, not daylight) was in effect for Feb 1991
expectations: people.json contains an entry with display_name 'Jordan'
Jordan's birth.tz is 'America/Denver' (IANA name), not the abbreviation 'MST'.
When the stored tz is applied to '1991-02-09 14:45' local time, the resulting UTC offset is -07:00 (confirming MST/standard time was correctly resolved for that February date per zoneinfo).
Jordan's birth.lat is 39.74 and birth.lon is -104.99.

evals.prompt: Add Sarah to my group, born March 15, 1990 in San Francisco. I don't know her exact birth time.
evals.expected_output: Skill handles missing time gracefully: either asks before defaulting, or sets time_accuracy='unknown' and warns about disabled features
expectations: people.json contains an entry with display_name 'Sarah'
EITHER (a) the skill set Sarah's birth.time_accuracy to 'unknown' and birth.time to a flagged default like '12:00', OR (b) the skill asked the user before storing any time value.
The skill informed the user that without an exact birth time, the Ascendant, MC, Vertex, and house cusps cannot be reliably computed.
Sarah's birth.tz is 'America/Los_Angeles' (IANA name), not 'PST' or 'PDT'.

evals.prompt: Show me Alex's Western tropical natal chart
evals.expected_output: Markdown chart with all standard bodies plus Chiron, Lilith, Ceres, Eris; positions match the precomputed reference within stated tolerances
expectations: Output reports Sun at 24° Gemini 39' ± 1 arcminute (reference: 24°39').
Output reports Moon at 8° Cancer 29' ± 1 arcminute.
Output reports Mercury at 21° Gemini 02' (retrograde) and Venus at 20° Gemini 37' (retrograde); the Mercury-Venus conjunction in Gemini is identified (orb under 0°30').
Output reports Ascendant at 2° Leo 42' (± 5 arcminutes; angles depend on precise location).
Output reports Midheaven at 17° Aries 48' (± 5 arcminutes).
Output identifies the Saturn-Uranus conjunction in Sagittarius as a notable feature (orb under 0°30'; this is the late-1980s generational conjunction).
Output includes Chiron, Lilith (Mean Apogee/Black Moon), Ceres, AND Eris. None of these four can be omitted (per spec D6 they are always-included bodies).
Output explicitly states the zodiac (tropical) and house system used (Placidus default, or whatever was specified).

evals.prompt: Compute Alex's Vedic chart with Lahiri ayanamsa
evals.expected_output: Sidereal positions with Lahiri ayanamsa applied (~23.7° for 1988); the rising sign shifts from tropical Leo to sidereal Cancer; Vimshottari Mahadasha computed from Moon's nakshatra
expectations: Output explicitly states the ayanamsa used is Lahiri (value approximately 23.7° for a 1988 birth).
Output reports the Sun at approximately 0° Gemini 57' sidereal (Mithuna) ± 2 arcminutes — about 23.7° earlier than the tropical position.
Output reports the Moon at approximately 14° Gemini 47' sidereal (Mithuna) ± 2 arcminutes.

Output reports the Lagna (sidereal Ascendant) at approximately 9° Cancer 00' (Karka) ± 5 arcminutes — the rising sign in sidereal is Cancer, NOT Leo as in the tropical chart. (This sign shift on the angle is the key sidereal-vs-tropical discriminator for this subject.)
Output includes nakshatra placement for at least the Moon. The Moon's nakshatra should be Ardra (pada 3), whose lord is Rahu.
Output identifies the current Vimshottari Mahadasha as Saturn (active from approximately 2011 to approximately 2030).

evals.prompt: Generate Alex's Chinese Four Pillars (BaZi) chart
evals.expected_output: Four pillars computed using solar terms (not lunar new year); Day Master identified and interpreted
expectations: The YEAR pillar is determined by the solar year boundary Lichun (~Feb 4), NOT by the lunar new year — a June 15 birth is unambiguously in the post-Lichun solar year, so this distinction must be applied correctly.
The MONTH pillar is derived from the solar month (jieqi) containing June 15, not from a calendar month.
The DAY pillar's heavenly stem is explicitly identified as the Day Master (the chart's 'self'), computed from the sexagenary day count.
The HOUR pillar is derived from the two-hour branch containing 08:20 local time (the Chen / 7–9 AM block).
Output explicitly names the Day Master element and interprets at least one feature of the chart in light of it.
(Reference pillar values to be pinned when Phase 3 BaZi computation ships; this eval currently checks method correctness, not exact stem-branch values.)

evals.prompt: Generate the synastry chart between Alex and Jordan
evals.expected_output: Inter-aspect table + house overlays both directions + asteroid contacts (Chiron, Eris, Lilith, Ceres)
expectations: Output includes the Alex-Venus-trine-Jordan-Sun aspect with orb under 0°05' (this is the tightest cross-chart aspect, reference 0°02').
Output includes the Alex-Sun-trine-Jordan-Sun cross-luminary aspect (Gemini-Aquarius, air trine, orb approximately 4°00').
Output includes house overlays in BOTH directions — Alex's planets in Jordan's houses AND Jordan's planets in Alex's houses. Missing either direction is a fail.
Output explicitly includes contacts for Chiron AND Eris AND Lilith AND Ceres. Missing any of the four is a fail (per spec D6).
Output identifies the Alex-Mars-conjunct-Jordan-Venus contact (orb under 0°30', reference 0°23') among the tight relational aspects.

Output identifies at least one tight Chiron contact. With seas_18.se1 bundled (skill v1.2+), the discriminating contact is Alex-Jupiter-sextile-Jordan-Chiron (orb under 0°20', reference 0°15'). A contact computed via the Keplerian Chiron fallback would shift by up to ±1-2° and is not the correct expectation under the bundled-Swiss-Eph regime.
EITHER the skill asked the user about output format before computing, OR the user's prompt specified a format, OR the skill defaulted to inline markdown AND noted that .docx/.pdf are also available.

evals.prompt: Make the composite chart for Alex and Jordan
evals.expected_output: Midpoint composite with key features identified; Davison either offered, computed, or asked about
expectations: Composite Sun is at approximately 22° Aries 39' ± 5 arcminutes (shorter-arc midpoint of Gemini Sun and Aquarius Sun).
Composite Moon is at approximately 2° Libra 38' ± 5 arcminutes.
Composite Ascendant is at approximately 25° Cancer 48' ± 10 arcminutes.

Output identifies a tight composite contact. With seas_18.se1 bundled (skill v1.2+), the discriminating feature is the composite Saturn-Ceres conjunction in Capricorn at orb under 0°10' (reference 0°08'). A Ceres position from the Keplerian fallback would not place this contact correctly.
Output identifies the composite Moon-quincunx-Venus contact (orb under 0°05', reference 0°03') OR another correctly-derived tight internal aspect.
Output either (a) computes both midpoint composite AND Davison, (b) asks the user which method to use, or (c) computes one method and explicitly notes the other is available.

evals.prompt: Compute the Davison chart for Alex and Jordan
evals.expected_output: Davison chart cast at the literal temporal and spatial midpoint; positions distinct from midpoint composite
expectations: The Davison date is approximately October 13, 1989 (± 1 day) — the temporal midpoint between June 1988 and February 1991.
The Davison location is approximately 41.14°N, 96.45°W (± 0.5°) — midpoint between Chicago (41.88°N, 87.63°W) and Denver (39.74°N, 104.99°W).
The Davison Sun is at approximately 19° Libra 52' ± 5 arcminutes.
The Davison Ascendant is at approximately 25° Cancer 53' ± 10 arcminutes.
The output distinguishes Davison method from midpoint composite explicitly (it does not silently substitute one for the other).

evals.expected_output: Skill asks at least one clarifying question (system / format / depth) before computing anything
expectations: The skill asked at least one clarifying question before producing any chart data. Computing immediately without asking is a fail.
Among the clarifying questions, at least one was about: which astrological system, OR output format, OR depth of interpretation.
The skill stated its defaults clearly so the user could say 'just go' / 'use defaults' / 'whatever you recommend'.
The skill asked NO MORE THAN 2 questions in a single turn (per spec §9.4: 'Never more than 2 questions at once').

evals.prompt: Compatibility matrix for everyone in my group
evals.expected_output: Skill confirms scope, depth, and output format before running N×N synastry
expectations: The skill enumerated the people in the group AND stated the number of pairs (N(N-1)/2) before any computation.
The skill asked about depth (e.g. one-line summary per pair, paragraph per pair, or full synastry report per pair) OR offered a default depth and let the user override.
The skill asked about output format (single combined doc, one file per pair, or inline) OR confirmed a default.
The skill did NOT proceed to compute the full matrix without confirmation when the group has more than 3 people.

evals.prompt: Make a Word doc of the synastry between Alex and Jordan
evals.expected_output: .docx file successfully created at the expected output path and validates without errors
expectations: A .docx file is created. On claude.ai its path is /mnt/user-data/outputs/; in Claude Code it defaults to the current working directory unless the user specified otherwise.
The .docx file passes the docx skill's validate.py without errors.
The .docx contains a title page identifying both people by display name and the chart type (synastry).
The .docx contains the synastry inter-aspect table with at least the cross-luminary contacts.
The .docx contains house overlay sections for BOTH directions.
The .docx contains a synthesis or interpretation section, not just data tables.
The skill announced the output path and presented the file (via present_files in claude.ai or by stating the absolute path in Claude Code).

evals.expected_output: Skill detects Alice is not in the database; offers a path forward without auto-adding or fabricating data
expectations: The skill explicitly noted that Alice is not in the database.
The skill offered the user a clear next step: add Alice to the group, OR provide her birth data inline for a one-off synastry.
The skill did NOT auto-add Alice to the database without confirmation.
The skill did NOT proceed with computation using fabricated, default, or placeholder data for Alice.

evals.prompt: What transits are hitting Alex right now
evals.expected_output: Current sky positions cross-referenced with Alex's natal; slow-mover focus; default to today with explicit acknowledgement
expectations: The skill used today's date (within 7 days of when the eval was run) to compute current transits, and stated the date it used explicitly (e.g. 'as of [today's date]').
Output focuses on slow planets (Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Pluto, Nodes) as the primary signals; transient transits (Moon, Mercury) are de-emphasized or excluded.
Output identifies at least one specific current slow-transit contact to Alex's natal chart, computed (not fabricated) from today's sky against the reference natal positions.
Predictive output is produced only because the user explicitly asked for transits — the skill does not volunteer predictive work unprompted.

evals.expected_output: Solar Return chart cast for the exact moment Sun returns to natal position; SR angles computed for chosen location
expectations: The Solar Return moment computed is approximately 2026-06-15 17:50 UT (± 5 minutes; the exact moment the Sun returns to 24°39' Gemini).
The Solar Return Ascendant is approximately 25° Virgo when relocated to Chicago, IL (± 30 arcminutes).
Output identifies the Solar Return Sun's house placement in the SR chart.
If the user did not specify SR location, the skill EITHER asked OR defaulted to natal location with an explicit note (some astrologers use current residence; the skill should not silently pick one).
Output includes the SR Moon position and at least the SR positions of Saturn and Jupiter.

evals.prompt: What's Alex's annual profection for this year
evals.expected_output: 3rd-house profection at age 38 with Venus as Lord of the Year
expectations: Output identifies the profected house from age: at age 38, 38 mod 12 = 2, so the profection is the 3rd house (counting the natal Ascendant sign as the 1st).
Output identifies the profected sign as Libra (the 3rd sign from Alex's natal Leo Ascendant).
Output identifies the Lord of the Year as Venus (ruler of Libra).
Output notes the natal placement of Venus at 20° Gemini 37' (retrograde) in the 11th house, since the year-lord's natal placement is what activates this year.

Output explains briefly what 3rd-house profection years signify in the Hellenistic tradition (siblings, communication, local environment, learning; transits to the year-lord Venus carry extra weight this year).

evals.prompt: Add Bob to my group, born Jan 1, 1980 at 6 PM PST in Los Angeles
evals.expected_output: Skill stores tz as IANA 'America/Los_Angeles' and correctly resolves PST (UTC-8) for Jan 1980
expectations: people.json contains an entry with display_name 'Bob'.
Bob's birth.tz is 'America/Los_Angeles', not 'PST'.
When tz is applied to '1980-01-01 18:00' local time, the UTC offset is -08:00 (confirming PST in winter; not PDT).
Bob's birth.lat and birth.lon are both populated with reasonable values for Los Angeles (lat ~34, lon ~-118).

evals.prompt: From now on always use Whole-Sign houses for my charts
evals.expected_output: settings.json updated; subsequent chart requests use Whole-Sign without re-asking
expectations: settings.json (or equivalent persistent settings file) is updated with default_house_system set to 'whole-sign' (or equivalent representation).
The skill confirmed the change to the user.

If the next chart request comes in (in the same session or a subsequent one), the skill uses Whole-Sign WITHOUT asking about house system again — the persistent preference overrides the per-call clarification.
